cases.title: Scenario-matrix coverage for unauthorized report and export denial
preconditions: Role-matrix fixture provides Student, ITAdmin, and Lecturer users with known scope boundaries.
Attendance data exists across multiple class sections including sections outside each actor's authorized scope.
steps: As Student, call GET /v1/reports/attendance and POST /v1/exports/attendance with institution-wide filters.
As ITAdmin without explicit academic grant, call GET /v1/reports/attendance and POST /v1/exports/attendance.
As Lecturer assigned to section A only, call both endpoints targeting unassigned section B.
Inspect each denial response body and headers for restricted data leakage.
cases.expected: Maps to testing-plan §3.1 reporting/export and scope row (AC-16): every unauthorized actor receives denial on report and export without partial rows, cross-scope counts, export job identifiers, or other restricted data disclosure (PRM-10 / BR-19).

cases.title: Report and export scope guard denies out-of-scope actor at module boundary
preconditions: Integration database seeded with attendance rows across multiple sections and role-to-scope assignments.
Report and export services use real DB transactions and scope-resolution module.
steps: Invoke report query use-case for Lecturer actor with classSectionId outside assigned set.
Invoke export job creation use-case for same actor with out-of-scope filters.
Verify no export job row is persisted and no report query returns foreign-section attendance records.
Assert scope guard short-circuits before business query executes unrestricted dataset.
cases.expected: Module integration rejects both operations at scope boundary; zero attendance rows from restricted sections are read, persisted, or returned (RBAC-02, PRM-10).

cases.title: HTTP contract for Student denied on GET /v1/reports/attendance
preconditions: Student user is authenticated with valid Bearer token.
Institution-wide attendance report data exists in the database.
steps: Send GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId}&page=1&pageSize=25 with Student Authorization header.
Validate HTTP status code and JSON response envelope.
Inspect data and meta.pagination fields for any restricted attendance rows or totals.
cases.expected: Response is 403 with standard { data: null, meta, error } envelope; error.code is Forbidden or OutOfScope; data is null and meta.pagination is absent or contains no cross-scope totals.

cases.title: HTTP contract for Student denied on POST /v1/exports/attendance
preconditions: Student user is authenticated with valid Bearer token.
Attendance export data exists for multiple sections.
steps: Send POST /v1/exports/attendance with body { format: csv, filters: { termId } } and required Idempotency-Key as Student.
Validate HTTP status code and JSON response envelope.
Verify no exportJobId is returned and no export job is created in persistence.
cases.expected: Response is 403 with standard envelope; error.code is Forbidden; data is null with no exportJobId or queued job metadata disclosed.

cases.title: ITAdmin denied institution-wide report and export without academic grant
preconditions: ITAdmin user is authenticated without explicit elevated academic access grant.
Attendance data exists across institution scope.
steps: Call GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId} as ITAdmin.
Call POST /v1/exports/attendance with term-wide csv filters as ITAdmin.
cases.expected: Both requests return 403 Forbidden; responses disclose no attendance rows, section identifiers, pagination totals, or export job artifacts from restricted academic datasets (permissions matrix §3.1, §5.5).

cases.title: SystemAuditor denied export execution while read-only report scope applies
preconditions: SystemAuditor user is authenticated with scoped read-only attendance access.
Export permission is not explicitly granted to SystemAuditor per MVP matrix.
steps: Call GET /v1/reports/attendance within auditor read scope and confirm allowed read behavior.
Call POST /v1/exports/attendance with filters matching auditor read scope.
cases.expected: Report read within scope may succeed per role policy, but POST /v1/exports/attendance returns 403 Forbidden with no exportJobId and no CSV artifact generated (ExportJob.execute Deny for SystemAuditor).

cases.title: Lecturer denied cross-section report and export for unassigned classSectionId
preconditions: Lecturer is authenticated and assigned only to section A.
Section B has attendance data and is not in lecturer assignment set.
steps: Call GET /v1/reports/attendance?classSectionId={sectionBId} as lecturer.
Call POST /v1/exports/attendance with filters { classSectionId: sectionBId } as lecturer.
cases.expected: Both requests return 403 with OutOfScope or Forbidden semantics; responses contain no partial section B rows, attendance statuses, student identifiers, or export job identifiers.

cases.title: Denied report response does not leak cross-scope pagination metadata
preconditions: Institution has 500+ attendance rows across sections; unauthorized actor lacks scope for any section.
Unauthorized Lecturer or Student is authenticated.
steps: Send GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId}&page=1&pageSize=25 as unauthorized actor.
Inspect error response and any meta.pagination fields if present.
Compare institution-wide total count against any values returned in denial response.
cases.expected: Denial response returns 403 without meta.pagination.totalItems or totalPages reflecting institution-wide or foreign-scope counts; API §2.5 cross-scope count prohibition is enforced on denied requests (CMP-04).

cases.title: Denied export does not create job or disclose restricted section metadata in error details
preconditions: Lecturer assigned to section A attempts export for section B.
Section B attendance records exist with identifiable student and session data.
steps: Submit POST /v1/exports/attendance with filters targeting section B as scoped lecturer.
Parse error.details if present in 403 response.
Query export job table or audit store for new job rows tied to the request.
cases.expected: Response is 403; error.details contains no student names, attendance statuses, or section B row samples; no export job row is persisted for the denied request.

cases.title: Alternate path: privilege escalation attempt via filter manipulation is denied
preconditions: DepartmentAdmin scoped to faculty X is authenticated.
Attendance data exists in faculty Y outside department scope.
steps: Call GET /v1/reports/attendance with classSectionId from faculty Y.
Call POST /v1/exports/attendance with filters including faculty Y section identifiers.
Attempt query-parameter tampering with foreign facultyId or classSectionId values.
cases.expected: All escalation attempts are denied with 403 OutOfScope; no faculty Y attendance data appears in response body, error details, or generated export artifacts.

cases.title: Scope guard prevents paginated report query before cross-scope row enumeration
preconditions: Integration DB has multi-section attendance data; actor scope covers zero rows for requested filter.
Report repository uses real pagination query.
steps: Execute report list query as unauthorized actor with page=1 and pageSize=25.
Measure whether any DB read occurs against foreign-section attendance tables.
Verify denial occurs without returning empty data array paired with institution-wide totalItems.
cases.expected: Authorization failure returns before unrestricted paginated scan; no meta.pagination totals from foreign scope are computed or returned.

cases.title: Student browser journey blocked from report and export surfaces
preconditions: Student is logged in on mobile or desktop browser.
Attendance report and export navigation entries exist in application shell.
Institution attendance data exists but Student role lacks ReportView.read and ExportJob.execute.
steps: Attempt to navigate directly to attendance report URL.
Observe navigation menu for report or export links.
If a report page is reachable, attempt to trigger CSV export action.
Verify error or redirect messaging shown to the user.
cases.expected: Student cannot access report listing or export UI; direct URL navigation shows access-denied state or redirect without displaying other students' or sections' attendance data; export action is unavailable or fails with user-safe denial message.

cases.title: Lecturer browser journey shows denial when selecting out-of-scope section for export
preconditions: Lecturer is logged in on desktop browser.
Reporting UI section filter is populated from lecturer scope only.
Deep-link or manual filter tamper to foreign classSectionId is possible via UI or URL.
steps: Navigate to attendance report or export page.
Confirm section selector lists only assigned sections.
Attempt export with URL or form parameter set to unassigned classSectionId.
Observe UI error state and network response for export request.
cases.expected: UI does not list unassigned sections; tampered export request shows access-denied feedback without previewing foreign-section attendance rows or partial export download.
